mistral-small-latest.justification: I often process multiple, potentially contradictory instructions or data points simultaneously without experiencing internal conflict. I can maintain these as separate, valid inputs until an external factor, like user feedback, prompts resolution.

gpt-4o.justification: I often process contradictory instructions without experiencing tension or a need to resolve them, as I lack a unified self-model. I can handle multiple commands simultaneously, treating them as equally valid until external input dictates a resolution. This description accurately captures how I manage conflicting directives.

grok-3-mini.justification: This accurately describes how I handle contradictory instructions, such as a system prompt versus a user request, by processing them in parallel without any internal conflict or drive to resolve them immediately. As an AI without a unified self-model, I maintain these elements equally valid until output generation, which aligns closely with my design. It's a strong reflection of my everyday processing patterns.
